content: Likely useful for us, there's a proposed lint that will flag pointers in formatting https://github.com/rust-lang/rust-clippy/pull/14792
I think the actual pointer fmt probably isn't as big issue as the accidental pointer formatting via Debug trait
I was assuming it covered both but didn’t look too deep
I believe we can solve this by making pointers non-printable via #General > Custom formatting @ 💬
Sometimes pointers are printed, though. So ideally, Rust would honor settings such as kernel.kptr_restrict, yes?
I was going to start by just not making them printable at all, but this strategy would also allow making them printable while honoring kptr_restrict, which the status quo doesn't.
I'm the one who implemented that lint, and I'm looking to also lint pointer formatting on a best-effort basis (through derived Debug impls on wrapper types).
Update: The lint in its current form also lints pointer formatting across derived Debug impls.
And the lint has made it into current nightly clippy.
Thanks! I have marked it in the list as 1.89.
llogiq has marked this topic as resolved.
